summary: Marked Yoshi's ftYs_SpecialHi.c as Matching in the build configuration and cleaned up nearby Yoshi static-header/data ownership. ftYs_SpecialHi.c stopped including the old static header and directly included ftYs_SpecialHi.h; the static header was renamed to ftYs_SpecialS.static.h and included by ftYs_SpecialS.c, removing an extern for ftYs_Unk3_803CED48. GALE01 sdata2 constants at 0x804D9A40-0x804D9A50 were changed from ftYs_Init_* globals to local @ labels. The decomp.dev bot reported +1836 linked-code bytes, +116 matched-data bytes, +24 linked-data bytes, and one new .data match for main/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialS.

key_files.change: Changed melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialHi.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Removed inclusion of ftYs_SpecialHi.static.h and added a direct include of ftYs_SpecialHi.h.
Added include of ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialS.static.h and removed extern bool ftYs_Unk3_803CED48[].
Renamed from ftYs_SpecialHi.static.h; removed the transitive ftYs_SpecialHi.h export include while keeping the static ftYs_Unk3_803CED48 data.
Reclassified sdata2 entries at 0x804D9A40, 0x804D9A44, 0x804D9A48, and 0x804D9A50 from named global ftYs_Init_* symbols to local @222, @223, @224, and @226 labels.

what_changed: Linked ftYs_SpecialHi.c by switching its configure.py object status from NonMatching to Matching.
Moved the static header association away from SpecialHi and onto SpecialS, matching the file name ftYs_SpecialS.static.h to the C file that includes it.
Removed a cross-file extern declaration for ftYs_Unk3_803CED48 in ftYs_SpecialS.c because the static header now provides the static bool array in that translation unit.
Stopped relying on ftYs_SpecialHi.static.h to export ftYs_SpecialHi.h; ftYs_SpecialHi.c now includes ftYs_SpecialHi.h directly.
Updated GALE01 symbol metadata so four sdata2 constants are treated as local compiler-generated @ labels instead of exported ftYs_Init_* globals.
Bot metrics reported linked code rising to 37.97% (+0.05%, +1836 bytes), matched data to 38.70% (+0.01%, +116 bytes), and linked data to 33.00% (+24 bytes).
smart_moves: Renaming ftYs_SpecialHi.static.h to ftYs_SpecialS.static.h reduced misleading ownership: the static bool table is now included by ftYs_SpecialS.c, where the diff also removed the previous extern declaration.
Adding ftYs_SpecialHi.h directly to ftYs_SpecialHi.c avoided depending on a static header for transitive public-header inclusion.
Changing the sdata2 symbols from global ftYs_Init_* names to local @ labels aligned the symbol map with apparent compiler-local constants, which is important for clean data matching.
The build-status flip in configure.py was kept minimal and paired with symbol/header fixes, making the PR a small link/match transition rather than a broad refactor.
decomp_lessons: When a translation unit becomes matching, update configure.py from Object(NonMatching, path) to Object(Matching, path) in the same PR as the source/symbol fixes.
Static headers should be named for the translation unit that owns/includes the static data; avoid leaving a .static.h attached to a neighboring file after data ownership becomes clear.
Avoid hiding required public headers behind .static.h files or IWYU export pragmas when the C file can include its own public header directly.
For local literal pools or compiler-generated constants in symbols.txt, use local @NNN labels and local scope rather than forcing descriptive global names when the evidence points to local data.
Data-section fixes in adjacent files can be part of linking a C file; this PR linked SpecialHi while the bot-reported new .data match was for ftYs_SpecialS.
naming_conventions: Yoshi fighter source/header files use the ftYs_ prefix and move suffixes such as ftYs_SpecialHi and ftYs_SpecialS.
Per-translation-unit static headers follow the module name plus .static.h, as shown by ftYs_SpecialS.static.h.
Unknown Yoshi data symbols in source keep address-bearing names such as ftYs_Unk3_803CED48, ftYs_Unk3_803CED84, and ftYs_Unk3_803CED94.
Local sdata2 symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt can use compiler-style @ labels such as @222, @223, @224, and @226 when not global.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote a file to Matching only after source/header/symbol ownership is consistent enough for the linker comparison.
Correct symbol scope in config/GALE01/symbols.txt for sdata2 constants to avoid false global names affecting data matching.
Use a static header to place static data inside the intended translation unit, replacing extern declarations when matching requires local data layout.
Preserve exact addresses and sizes in the symbol map while changing only symbol names/scope: 0x804D9A40 and 0x804D9A44 are 4-byte floats; 0x804D9A48 and 0x804D9A50 are 8-byte doubles.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The PR body was empty, so the rationale is inferred from the diff and decomp.dev bot report.
decomp-dev bot reported one new match: main/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialS .data improved from 65.12% to 100.00% (+41 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that fully match or link ftYs_SpecialS.c after the ftYs_SpecialS.static.h ownership cleanup.
Search for remaining ftYs_Init_* sdata2 globals that may actually be local @ labels.
Check other Yoshi special-move files for static headers included by the wrong translation unit.
Audit .static.h files that export public headers via IWYU pragma rather than having the .c include its public header directly.
